3) Timeline & priority: are you looking for a pilot in 2 weeks / 1 month / 2+ months?

To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@findly.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: 

- Goal: replace heuristic reordering in our search results with a lightweight reranker for top 50 candidates; target improvement: +10–15% NDCG@10 and measurable reduction in user re-clicks.
- Data: we can provide ~2.5M query -> candidate lists (JSONL) with click labels for a rolling 90d window. Average candidates per query ~120, median 48.
- Security: we need an overview of how Redwood handles PII in logs, where embeddings are stored, and whether keys can be scoped to a VPC/private deployment.
- Integration asks (high level): SDK vs HTTP direct; auth options; support for struc...

Subject: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive

Hi Alex / Priya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap: Novacorp is prioritizing a live technical deep dive to validate inference throughput for your mixed-traffic (short chat + long-form summarization) use cases, and you asked for a schedule that rotates across US/EU/APAC-friendly times so product teams in each region can join periodically.

Rather than a single fixed time, we often run a 3-band "Commuter Bands" approach that alternates who gets the most convenient slot on a weekly basis. It looks like this (example):

- Band A (US-friendly): Tue 10:00 PT / 13:00 ET / Wed 02:00 SGT
- Band B (EU-friendly): Wed 15:00 CET / 09:00 ET / Thu 00:00 SG...

Subject: SaaSFlow: slab pricing simulation & commit phasing

Ethan —

Thanks again for the deep dive today. Per our conversation I attached a couple of slab simulations so you can see the unit economics across different commit shapes. The attached slab_simulation_v2.xlsx includes:
- Monthly token bands (0-500M, 500M-2B, 2B+)
- Per-token rate at each band and effective blended rates for 3 commit sizes
- A model for 6-month ramp (0% month 1 -> 100% month 6) and a 12-month steady commit

Key assumptions called out in the sheet: throughput smoothing, auto-burst cap (25%), and prefix cache savings (estimated 12%).

A few things to highlight up front:
- We can offer a slab discount starting at 15% off hosted baseline for a ~1B token/mo commit.

text:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Ng <sarah@hyperstack.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 15:03:00 +0000
Subject: Hyperstack evaluation — unit economics & proposed test matrix

Sarah — thanks for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a concrete test plan we can run next week.

Recap (what we heard)
- Primary evaluation lens: unit cost per 1M tokens under realistic production mix (short chat messages + occasional long generations).
- Constraints: sub-150ms median latency target, p95 < 400ms, and ability to preserve conversational state (KV cache) for prompt reuse.
- Hyperscaler interest: cheaper headline price but unclear how caching, batching, and model variants affect real-world effective cost.

Redwood baseline (ballpark numbers for planning)
- Hosted rwd-small / rwd-medium options: baseline modeled cost ~ $25–...

- Gate A (end week 5): If pilot passes acceptance criteria, customer commits to 3-month reserved capacity at 40% discount vs on-demand.
- Week 6–12: Ramp to target throughput and P95 latency SLOs; incremental discounts unlocked at 60% and 80% utilization checkpoints.

- Latency target: p95 < 1.5s, p99 < 2.5s

Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t...

text:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ...
